key_insight: RHEED-derived machine learning features can separate high- and low-quality GaSe MBE growths and help predict film quality more effectively than process parameters alone.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical pathway toward real-time, closed-loop control of molecular beam epitaxy to speed optimization of thin-film synthesis.
method: Unsupervised learning on in situ RHEED patterns to learn growth-state embeddings and classify sample quality.
Mutual information analysis to relate RHEED embeddings and growth conditions to XRD rocking-curve FWHM and AFM roughness.
Supervised models for FWHM and RMS roughness prediction, strengthened by adding RHEED embeddings; residual-based anomaly detection for outlier growths.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a strong association between RHEED embeddings and XRD rocking-curve FWHM, while supervised prediction improves when RHEED features are included.
watch_item: The model performance is only moderate, so robust real-time deployment will require additional validation across more growth conditions and datasets.
paper_title: Multi-modal machine learning analysis of GaSe molecular beam epitaxy growth conditions
